problem_statement: Expected: a fleet inspection recorded at 14:03 UTC is accepted by the regional synchronization service within its two-minute future-skew allowance. Observed: one kiosk's inspection is rejected as eleven minutes in the future while a second kiosk accepts an equivalent inspection. Diagnose the causal family using read-only measurements and isolated validation only.

observations.statement: The immutable ingest receipt records server_received_at=14:03:22Z, inspection_recorded_at=14:14:08Z, rejection=future_timestamp, kiosk=K-17, and a permitted skew of 120 seconds.
A read-only signed clock comparison captured during the incident places kiosk K-17 at +10m46s relative to two independent UTC references, while kiosk K-18 is within 180ms.

An isolated validator holds the captured payload, service revision, settings, data snapshot, and dependency responses fixed; substituting the measured reference time for the kiosk timestamp changes the outcome from future_timestamp to accepted.
Both kiosks report the same signed application revision, settings fingerprint, and operating-system image even though only K-17 exhibits the rejection.
A captured service-health record shows normal request latency and successful equivalent submissions from K-18 during the same minute, despite an earlier regional connectivity warning.
